titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the George G. Phelps Family Papers
titlestmt.author: Compiled by Staff of Manuscripts and Archives under the supervision of Francis Radvonovsky

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Thursday October 11, 2018 at 07:00

did.abstract: Correspondence and business papers of George G. Phelps, farmer and businessman, and other members of the Phelps family of Hebron, Meriden and Wallingford, Connecticut.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: George G. Phelps Family Papers (MS 17). Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from George L. Gaines, Jr., in 1942.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: I. Correspondence. II. General Subject Files.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The papers of George G. Phelps and other members of the Phelps Family of Hebron, Meriden and Wallingford, Connecticut, consist primarily of family letters, business letters and papers for the period 1838-1928. Most of the correspondence is concentrated in the period 1855-1859 and concerns Mr. Phelps' farm in Manchester, various real estate interests in Hebron, Grand Lodge matters and proceedings on financial notes. The 173 piece collection was purchased from George L. Gaines, Jr., in 1942, and is of limited historical interest, providing some information on the business of farming in Connecticut in the mid-nineteenth century and on the local history of the towns discussed in the letters.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Unpublished materials authored or otherwise produced by the creator(s) of this collection are in the public domain. There are no restrictions on use. Copyright status for other collection materials is unknown. Transmission or reproduction of materials protected by U.S. Copyright Law (Title 17, U.S.C.) beyond that allowed by fair use requires the written permission of the copyright owners. Works not in the public domain cannot be commercially exploited without permission of the copyright owners. Responsibility for any use rests exclusively with the user.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence contains family and business letters written to George G. Phelps, Mary Page Phelps, Elizabeth Phelps, Harriet C. Phelps, and Emma K. Foskett. The letters are arranged alphabetically by name of author for each member of the family.
Topics covered in letters include George G. Phelps' farm in Wallingford, real estate interests and Grand Lodge matters.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series includes appointment certificates, minutes, Grand Lodge communications, receipts and printed material.

did.unittitle: Newspaper Clipping about Escaped Slave Family
